summary: Gruber announces a new feature for Daring Fireball: the Linked List, a daily collection of interesting links with brief commentary. The key innovation is its distribution model — the RSS feed is exclusively available to paying Daring Fireball members, while web archives are free but delayed by one day. He positions it as a members-only perk rather than a punishment for non-members. The launch is timed to coincide with Apple's WWDC announcements.
key_points: The Linked List is a new linkblog format — frequent but not frenetic short-form link commentary alongside Daring Fireball's longer essays
The RSS feed is members-only, establishing an early model of paid content gating tied to RSS rather than paywalled web pages
Web archives are free but delayed by one day, creating a timeliness incentive to subscribe rather than a hard paywall
Gruber frames the restriction as an added benefit for members rather than a restriction on non-members — a deliberate positioning choice
money_quotes: Call it a "link log", or "linkblog", or just "a good way to dick around on the Internet for a few minutes a day".
The one-day delay isn't intended to be punitive toward non-members; the idea is more that the Linked List is intended as a nice members-only extra.
key_insight: Gruber pioneered the linkblog format and an early model of indie content monetization by making timeliness — not access — the premium feature.
